pysoniq.io¶

Audio file I/O

class pysoniq.io.Signal(y, sr)[source]¶

Bases: object

pysoniq.io.load_audio(filepath)[source]¶

Load audio file

Parameters:

filepath – str or Path to audio file

Returns:

numpy array (normalized float32, -1 to 1) samplerate: int, sample rate in Hz

Return type:

audio

Supports: .wav, .mp3

pysoniq.io.load_segment(filepath, start_sec, end_sec)[source]¶

Load a time-bounded segment from an audio file without reading the full file.

Implements lazy loading for long recordings (10-20+ minutes). Only the frame range corresponding to [start_sec, end_sec] is read from disk. No normalization is applied beyond the bit-depth conversion in the underlying loader. Caller is responsible for mono conversion if needed.

Parameters:

  • filepath – str or Path to the audio file (.wav or .mp3).

  • start_sec – float, segment start time in seconds.

  • end_sec – float, segment end time in seconds.

Returns:

numpy array (float32, shape (n_samples,) or (n_samples, n_channels)) samplerate: int, sample rate in Hz.

Return type:

audio

Raises:

  • ValueError – if end_sec <= start_sec or file format is unsupported.

  • RuntimeError – if ffmpeg is unavailable for MP3 files.

pysoniq.io.load_signal(filepath)[source]¶
pysoniq.io.save_audio(filepath, audio, samplerate)[source]¶

Save audio to file

Parameters:

  • filepath – str or Path to output file

  • audio – numpy array (float32, -1 to 1)

  • samplerate – int, sample rate in Hz

Supports: .wav

pysoniq
